author: jsign
content:
Does this "witness generation" mostly means reading MPT nodes to construct the MPT proofs for the `ExecutionWitness.state` field? That's my theoretical guess considering: i) `ExecutionWitness.headers` have a max cap of 256 entries, so this could always live in memory and picking what's required be basically free (i.e no IO acces required for generating it). ii) The `ExecutionWitness.codes` should be basically free since as the block is executed collecting this is basically free. So this is why I suspect when you say "execution witness" being the bottleneck now, I suspect is mostly related to the MPT proofs for each state access for `ExecutionWitness.state`. Is this the case?
(great work btw!)

author: jsign
content:
As a separate thought: I think whenever BALs are ready, this `ExecutionWitness.state` part can be started to be constructed as soon as the block starts executing; since in theory we already know the accounts and storage slots that were accessed. And also since the BALs allow post-state root calculation to be done in parallel, we can also collect faster potential extra `state` items from post-state root branch compressions. 
I actually think today (without BALs) this `ExecutionWitness.state`  MPT proof collection heavy IO can be pipelined during block execution (i.e. start picking the MPT proof nodes as soon as the state access was detected during block exec), but if that isn't the case today might not be worth doing it until BAL exists which might make everything easier to do. (Just thinking out loud here a bit)
